tweets.text:
Final crude estimate for July 10 week.

Total crude draw of 6.1 million bbls. SPR release slowed to 3 million bbls, with the current release rate coinciding with a floor of 300 million bbls.

EIA owes us over 10 million bbls. We expect EIA's crude storage figure to be higher than our figure of -3.1 million bbls. We see EIA reporting -7 million bbls. Total crude draw could be closer to 10 million bbls.

API is going to be bearish relative to EIA. If API reports -3 million bbls, EIA will report closer to -7 to -8 million bbls.
tweets.full_text:
Final crude estimate for July 10 week.

Total crude draw of 6.1 million bbls. SPR release slowed to 3 million bbls, with the current release rate coinciding with a floor of 300 million bbls.

EIA owes us over 10 million bbls. We expect EIA's crude storage figure to be higher than our figure of -3.1 million bbls. We see EIA reporting -7 million bbls. Total crude draw could be closer to 10 million bbls.

API is going to be bearish relative to EIA. If API reports -3 million bbls, EIA will report closer to -7 to -8 million bbls.

Spotting Market Bubbles 
Almost every day, someone in the media claims the market is in a bubble.  It may be true, but history has a brutal history of calling market bubbles too early, costing investors dearly.  We discuss the "four horsemen" to watch and how to navigate the market currently.

tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours: war on.

No visible inbound tankers in the last 24 hours.

Reports stated that Iran fired at two vessels today. There was an escort that saw tankers exit, ATARAKTOS, was one of the VLCCs that exited.

With no diplomatic resolution for the Oman lane, we will likely see more escalation throughout this week. 

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic and my inbound tanker list.
